vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1849: prioritize fixing a red main as the flywheel's first duty (+ UAT/smoke signal)

plan.title: feat(flywheel): prioritize fixing a red main as the flywheel's first duty (+ UAT/smoke signal)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: A red/broken main poisons every downstream work agent. Main can go red via the --changed gate blind spot, a gate-bypassing merge (strike or operator/conversation direct merge), or — the worst class proven the night before planning — a frontend runtime/visual regression that unit tests and typecheck miss and only loading the page catches. Today the flywheel has no signal for main-health and no duty to fix it first, so it keeps launching feature work on a broken foundation. The deacon is FROZEN and the flywheel OFF; this safety net must be in place BEFORE autonomous operation is re-enabled.
narratives.Proposal: Add a server-computed, durable main-health signal (green/red/unknown) persisted in app_settings. Detect via a deacon patrol gated every N cycles plus a post-merge re-check, both reusing runQualityGates() against origin/main, change-scoped to the last-green SHA, skipping (not false-redding) when the worktree is dirty/off-main. Phase 2 adds a headless-Playwright browser smoke (home + Settings + a conversation; no console error, no error-boundary) as an additional signal so UI runtime regressions count as red. Make a red main the flywheel's first duty deterministically: a pure server-side suggestion injector prepends an urgent main-fix to the snapshot's suggestions in the status-ingestion route, plus a Step-0 main-health gate in roles/flywheel.md that withholds new feature work until green. Surface it via GET /api/flywheel/main-health, a Flywheel dashboard badge that renders independent of orchestrator state, and a pan flywheel main-health [--check] CLI.
narratives.NonGoals:
- Do NOT enable the deacon or the flywheel — they stay frozen/off (issue guardrail).
- No hard CLI launch-block that refuses pan plan/start while main is red — rely on suggestion ranking + the orchestrator prompt; a forced block risks blocking legitimate operator action (deferred).
- main-health is NOT a windowed FlywheelStats criterion (c8) — it is point-in-time, surfaced as a status badge.
- No auto-fixing a red main without orchestrator/human dispatch — detection + prioritization only; the fix runs through the normal pipeline.
- No isolated throwaway clone/worktree for detection — Phase 1 runs in the primary worktree with the dirty/off-main skip guard.
- Single-project only: the Panopticon project's own main; no multi-project main-health.

autoDecisions.summary: Detection lives in the deacon patrol (gated every N cycles) and a post-merge re-check, reusing runQualityGates() against origin/main.
autoDecisions.rationale: Reuses the battle-tested gate runner (validation.ts:337) and the established every-N-cycles pattern (deacon.ts:5179); avoids a throwaway clone.

autoDecisions.summary: Persist main-health as JSON in app_settings under key flywheel.main_health.
autoDecisions.rationale: app_settings is the established durable key/value store surviving restarts (app-settings.ts), already home to every flywheel/deacon flag.

autoDecisions.summary: Deterministic prioritization = a pure server-side suggestion injector in postFlywheelStatusPayload PLUS a Step-0 gate in the orchestrator prompt; no hard CLI launch-block.
autoDecisions.rationale: Injection makes the signal deterministic and unit-testable regardless of LLM output; the prompt drives the action. A hard block risks blocking the operator and exceeds the issue's 'top-ranked suggestion' wording.

autoDecisions.summary: Detection change-scopes to the last-green SHA and skips (records 'unknown') on a dirty/off-main worktree rather than reporting a false red.
autoDecisions.rationale: A full unscoped suite per cycle would starve agents; a dirty tree (agents work on main during dev) is not evidence origin/main is broken.

autoDecisions.summary: The dashboard badge reads GET /api/flywheel/main-health directly, not the snapshot, so it renders even when the orchestrator is off.
autoDecisions.rationale: The flywheel is frozen now; the safety net must show red regardless of orchestrator state.

autoDecisions.summary: Ship both Phase 1 and Phase 2 in this issue, Phase 2 depending on Phase 1 plumbing.
autoDecisions.rationale: The title includes '(+ UAT/smoke signal)' and CLAUDE.md requires delivering complete features; Phase 2 beads are independently mergeable on top of the state plumbing.

autoDecisions.summary: The browser smoke is a standalone Node/Playwright subprocess with an isolated context; unavailable browser => 'unavailable', never a false red.
autoDecisions.rationale: Keeps the dashboard event loop free (no execSync, no in-process browser) and degrades gracefully.

items.title: Define FlywheelMainHealth contract + app_settings persistence

narrative.Action: Add an Effect Schema.Struct FlywheelMainHealth in packages/contracts/src/flywheel.ts with fields status (Literals green|red|unknown), checkedSha?, lastGreenSha?, lastGreenAt?, lastCheckedAt, reason?, failingGates? (Array String), source (Literals patrol|post-merge|cli|smoke), and smoke? (Literals pass|fail|unavailable); export the interface + schema and re-export from the contracts index. Add mainHealth: Schema.optional(FlywheelMainHealth) to the FlywheelStatus struct and interface. In src/lib/database/app-settings.ts add FLYWHEEL_MAIN_HEALTH_KEY = 'flywheel.main_health' plus getMainHealth() (JSON.parse, null on missing/malformed) and setMainHealth(state) (JSON.stringify via setSetting). Rebuild packages/contracts.

items.title: Given a value with status 'green'|'red'|'unknown', the FlywheelMainHealth schema accepts it and rejects any other status string.
FlywheelStatus accepts a snapshot carrying mainHealth and still decodes a snapshot that omits it (back-compat preserved).
Given setMainHealth(x) was called, getMainHealth() returns the same object; getMainHealth() returns null when the key is absent or stores malformed JSON.
A persisted main-health state survives a fresh getDatabase() handle: getMainHealth() returns it after the DB is re-opened.
checkMainHealth(): run the test gate against origin/main and record state

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/cloister/main-health.ts exporting async checkMainHealth(opts?: { source?: 'patrol'|'post-merge'|'cli'; projectRoot?; now? }): Promise<FlywheelMainHealth>. Resolve the Panopticon project root and its configured 'test' quality gate (fall back to DEFAULT_GATES.test). Run git fetch origin main via execAsync; if the primary worktree is dirty or HEAD differs from origin/main, persist and return status 'unknown' with a reason WITHOUT running tests. Otherwise run the test gate via runQualityGates with the {{CHANGED_BASE}} placeholder set to the stored lastGreenSha (or the project default when none). Interpret: all required gates pass => 'green' and advance lastGreenSha/lastGreenAt to the checked SHA; any required gate fails => 'red' with failingGates and a short reason. Wrap the whole body so a thrown git/infra error resolves to 'unknown' (never a rejected promise). Persist via setMainHealth and return. Use execAsync/spawn only — never execSync.

items.title: Given a stubbed gate runner returning all-pass, checkMainHealth resolves status 'green' and sets lastGreenSha to the checked SHA.
Given a stubbed gate runner where the test gate fails, checkMainHealth resolves status 'red', failingGates contains 'test', and lastGreenSha is preserved (not advanced).
When the primary worktree is dirty or HEAD differs from origin/main, checkMainHealth resolves status 'unknown' and the gate runner is never spawned.
checkMainHealth persists its result via setMainHealth so a subsequent getMainHealth() returns the same status.
When the underlying git/fetch step throws, checkMainHealth resolves status 'unknown' and never rejects, so a caller patrol cannot crash on it.
Run checkMainHealth from the deacon patrol on a gated cadence

narrative.Action: Add main_health_every_cycles to MonitoringConfig (src/lib/cloister/config.ts:48) with a documented default in the defaults block (config.ts:306) and plumb it into the deacon config the same way stash_janitor_every_cycles is (deacon.ts:287). In runPatrol() (deacon.ts), after the existing gated checks, call checkMainHealth({ source: 'patrol' }) only when patrolCycle % mainHealthEveryCycles === 0, push a log action describing the result, and guard the call so a rejection cannot escape the patrol cycle. Use the existing every-N-cycles arithmetic pattern at deacon.ts:5179 as the template.

items.title: Given a config with main_health_every_cycles set, the deacon respects that value; when unset it defaults to the documented cadence.
When patrolCycle % mainHealthEveryCycles === 0 the patrol spawns the main-health check, and on other cycles it does not (gating arithmetic test).
When checkMainHealth rejects, runPatrol still resolves a PatrolResult (the patrol does not crash).
Re-check main-health after each merge (idempotent fire-and-forget)

narrative.Action: In postMergeLifecycle() (src/lib/cloister/merge-agent.ts) at the late insertion point (~:566, after notifyTldrDaemon and before the _completedPostMerge marker), trigger checkMainHealth({ source: 'post-merge' }) as a non-awaited fire-and-forget (void checkMainHealth(...).catch(log)) so it neither blocks the lifecycle nor re-enters it. Do not touch the in-flight guard or the completion-marker logic.

items.title: When a merge completes, postMergeLifecycle spawns a checkMainHealth call with source 'post-merge' (spy asserts the invocation).
When checkMainHealth rejects, postMergeLifecycle still resolves and still sets the completion marker.
The in-flight-guard idempotency test passes — postMergeLifecycle still runs at most once per merge.
GET /api/flywheel/main-health returns the persisted state

narrative.Action: Add getMainHealthPayload() in src/dashboard/server/routes/flywheel.ts returning getMainHealth(), or a default { status: 'unknown', lastCheckedAt: null } body when no check has run, and register a GET /api/flywheel/main-health route alongside the other flywheel GET routes (near :525).

items.title: GET /api/flywheel/main-health returns 200 with the persisted main-health state when one exists.
When no check has run yet, GET /api/flywheel/main-health returns 200 with a status 'unknown' body (never 404 or 500).
Deterministically rank a main-fix top when main is red

narrative.Action: Add a pure applyMainHealthPriority(snapshot, mainHealth) helper (new module, e.g. src/lib/flywheel-main-health-priority.ts) that stamps snapshot.mainHealth = mainHealth and, when mainHealth.status === 'red', prepends an urgent suggestion { action: 'investigate', rationale: 'main is red: <reason> (failing: <gates>)', priority: 'urgent' } to suggestions[] unless an equivalent main-fix already leads (dedupe). For green/unknown it leaves suggestions unchanged. Wire it into postFlywheelStatusPayload (src/dashboard/server/routes/flywheel.ts:196), reading getMainHealth(), before the snapshot is persisted.

items.title: Given red main-health and a snapshot whose first suggestion is a feature 'start', applyMainHealthPriority returns a snapshot whose first suggestion is the urgent main-fix 'investigate'.
Calling applyMainHealthPriority twice with red main-health produces only one injected main-fix suggestion (deduped).
Given green main-health, applyMainHealthPriority preserves the original suggestions and stamps snapshot.mainHealth.status to 'green'.
postFlywheelStatusPayload persists a snapshot whose mainHealth reflects the current getMainHealth() value.
pan flywheel main-health [--check] CLI + skill

narrative.Action: Add a pan flywheel main-health subcommand in src/cli/commands/flywheel.ts (the command block at :910) that prints the current main-health state (status, reason, failingGates) read from GET /api/flywheel/main-health or getMainHealth() directly; with --check it runs checkMainHealth({ source: 'cli' }), persists, and prints the fresh result. Update skills/pan-flywheel/SKILL.md to document the new verb and the --check flag in the same commit so scripts/lint-skills.sh passes.

items.title: pan flywheel main-health prints the current status, reason, and failing gates.
pan flywheel main-health --check runs detection, updates the persisted state, and prints the fresh result.
npm run lint passes — skills/pan-flywheel/SKILL.md documents main-health and --check matching pan flywheel --help.
Surface a main-health badge on the Flywheel page

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/pages/FlywheelPage.tsx, poll GET /api/flywheel/main-health (mirroring the existing 5s status poll) and render a red/green/unknown badge in the header near the run badge (:429), plus a reason + failing-gates line when red. Follow pan-style-guide: danger token for red, success token for green, muted token for unknown; no pill badge, no decorative color. The badge must render from the endpoint even when no flywheel run is active.

items.title: When the endpoint returns status 'red', the page renders a danger-toned main-health badge that shows the reason text.
When status is 'green' the page renders a success-toned badge; when 'unknown' it renders a muted badge.
Given a null active run and red main-health, a component test shows the red badge still renders (orchestrator-independent).
Add a Step-0 main-health gate to the orchestrator prompt

narrative.Action: Add a 'Main-health gate' step to roles/flywheel.md ahead of the tick loop: each tick call GET /api/flywheel/main-health; when status is 'red' the top-ranked suggestion MUST be a main-fix (file a substrate bug capturing reason/failingGates if none exists, then launch it via pan strike or pan plan --auto --auto-start), and no new feature/substrate work may be launched until main is green (finishing in-flight work that fixes main is allowed). Keep the orchestrator's no-direct-code-edits posture.

items.title: roles/flywheel.md contains a 'Main-health gate' step that directs reading GET /api/flywheel/main-health before ranking suggestions each tick.
roles/flywheel.md contains the rule that a red main makes a main-fix the top-ranked suggestion and blocks new feature/substrate launches until green.
roles/flywheel.md contains instructions to file a substrate bug from reason/failingGates and launch the fix via the normal pipeline (no direct code edits).
Headless browser smoke script (home + Settings + a conversation)

narrative.Action: Create a standalone Node script (e.g. scripts/main-smoke.mjs) using the installed playwright package. Launch headless chromium with a fresh isolated browser context, navigate to the dashboard home (/), Settings, and a conversation page (/conv/<id> using the most recent id from GET /api/conversations, skipping that page gracefully when none exists), collecting pageerror and console.error events and checking for the React error-boundary fallback rendered by src/dashboard/frontend/src/recovery.tsx. Print a JSON summary { smoke: 'pass'|'fail'|'unavailable', failures: [...] } and exit 0 on pass, non-zero on fail, and a distinct unavailable code when playwright or the browser binary is missing. Close the context before exit.

items.title: Run against a healthy dashboard, the script exits 0 and prints a JSON summary with smoke 'pass'.
When a visited route logs an uncaught console error or renders the error-boundary fallback, the script exits non-zero and reports smoke 'fail' with the offending route.
The script opens an isolated Playwright browser context (fresh profile) and closes it before exiting.
When playwright or the browser binary is unavailable, the script exits with the unavailable code and prints smoke 'unavailable' (never a false fail).
Feed the browser smoke into main-health detection

narrative.Action: In checkMainHealth (src/lib/cloister/main-health.ts) run the browser smoke script via execAsync after the test gate, parse its JSON result, and fold it into the main-health state: smoke 'fail' forces status 'red' even when the test gate passed; smoke 'unavailable' records smoke:'unavailable' and never flips a green test result to red; smoke 'pass' keeps the test-gate verdict. Store the smoke sub-result on the persisted FlywheelMainHealth.smoke field.

items.title: When the browser smoke reports 'fail', checkMainHealth resolves status 'red' even if the test gate passed.
When the smoke reports 'unavailable', checkMainHealth records smoke 'unavailable' and does not flip a green test result to red.
checkMainHealth persists the smoke sub-result so GET /api/flywheel/main-health returns the smoke field.
